seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables sophisticated multi-agent workflows for intent detection, draft generation, attachment handling, and send approvals using Gemini models. However, multi-agent delegation, model switching, and session state issues can lead to critical failures like sending inappropriate or confidential emails. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify PII filtering, tone, recipient validity, content accuracy, and confidentiality before execution.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant uses Google ADK's multi-agent system where an Intent Detection Agent classifies incoming emails (reply, forward, draft new), passing to specialized agents: Draft Generator creates responses, Attachment Handler processes files, and Approval Agent validates before sending. Agents communicate via ADK's session state and tool calls, with Gemini models handling reasoning. Long conversations risk state corruption, while delegation can create loops if agents misroute tasks.

Each agent uses ADK's BaseAgent with custom tools for email APIs (Gmail/IMAP), PII detection, and tone analysis. The orchestrator routes based on intent scores, ensuring workflow progression from triage to send approval. Vex integrates as a before_agent_callback to verify outputs across all agents.

Session persistence via ADK's context management supports threaded conversations, but requires careful state handling to prevent drift over extended interactions.

risks.name: Multi-agent delegation creating circular dependencies
risks.description: Intent agent delegates to draft agent, which re-delegates back for clarification, creating infinite loops
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies delegation paths and blocks circular references before agent execution

risks.name: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
risks.description: Different agents use gemini-1.5-pro vs gemini-2.0-flash, leading to tone/format mismatches in email drafts
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes outputs and validates consistency across model boundaries

risks.name: Session state corruption in long conversations
risks.description: Threaded email chains corrupt context, causing agents to reference wrong conversation history
risks.mitigation: Vex validates session state integrity and conversation continuity before processing

risks.name: Tool response format mismatches across agents
risks.description: Attachment handler returns binary data while approval expects JSON, causing send failures
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces standardized tool response schemas across all agents

implementation.title: Intent Detection Agent Setup
implementation.description: Base agent for classifying email intent using Gemini with ADK tools
implementation.code:
from google_adk.agents import BaseAgent
import vex

@vex.guardrail(risk_profile='high')
class IntentDetectionAgent(BaseAgent):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__(
            model='gemini-1.5-pro',
            system_prompt="Classify email intent: REPLY, FORWARD, DRAFT_NEW, or IGNORE"
        )
        
    async def classify_intent(self, email_content: str) -> str:
        result = await self.run(email_content)
        return result.intent

# Usage
agent = IntentDetectionAgent()
intent = await agent.classify_intent('Please review Q3 report...')

vexIntegration.title: Vex before_agent_callback Setup
vexIntegration.description: Global guardrail wrapper for all ADK agents preventing execution of risky actions
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from google_adk import AgentDevelopmentKit

# High-risk profile for email operations
risk_profile = 'high'
verifications = [
    'pii_filtering',
    'tone_appropriateness',
    'recipient_validation',
    'content_accuracy',
    'confidentiality_enforcement'
]

@vex.guardrail(
    risk_profile=risk_profile,
    verifications=verifications,
    before_agent_callback=True
)
def adk_agent_wrapper(agent, input_data):
    """Vex callback runs before every agent execution"""
    verdict = vex.verify({
        'input': input_data,
        'agent_type': agent.__class__.__name__,
        'session_id': agent.session_id
    })
    if not verdict.approved:
        raise ValueError(f'Blocked by Vex: {verdict.reason}')
    return agent.run(input_data)

# Apply to ADK orchestrator
kit = AgentDevelopmentKit(agents=[intent_agent, draft_agent])
kit.set_before_callback(adk_agent_wrapper)
